76028.subject: Crypto Biz: When dollars disappear, stablecoins step in
76028.body:
Crypto Biz Newsletter

Автор: Cointelegraph


Автор: Cointelegraph

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/crypto-biz-when-dollars-disappear-stablecoins-step-cointelegraph-3nkrc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BfJUsQ1LDSVu2zuHOn9%2FdvQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0G-qxYqY3PLsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrn6d09v~28-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrn6d09v-28
  
Stablecoins have long been pitched as a faster way to move dollars across borders. In Bolivia, they’re increasingly becoming a way to access dollars in the first place. The country’s recent proposal to recognize Tether’s USDt (USDT) for payments underscores how economic instability is driving adoption in many emerging markets.  Elsewhere, Bitcoin miners are discovering that pivoting to AI infrastructure may unlock new revenue streams, but it doesn’t shield them from investor scrutiny. Bolivia weighs recognizing USDT amid dollar shortage Bolivia is considering a regulatory framework that would recognize Tether’s USDT as a payment currency, marking another step in the country’s push to integrate digital assets into its financial system. Economy and Public Finance Minister Jose Gabriel Espinoza said the proposal would allow USDT to circulate alongside the boliviano and the US dollar for payments and savings. The framework remains under review and would include anti-money laundering safeguards, as Bolivia is still on the Financial Action Task Force’s gray list. The initiative follows the lifting of the country’s crypto ban in 2024 and the new administration’s pledge to expand access to digital asset services. The proposal comes as Bolivia struggles with a prolonged shortage of US dollars after pressure on foreign exchange reserves forced the government to abandon its long-standing currency peg earlier this year. The resulting gap between the official and parallel exchange rates has increased demand for dollar-denominated alternatives such as USDT, which has become an increasingly popular payment tool in the country. Source: EL DEBER Continue Reading… Bitcoin miners’ AI pivot draws scrutiny over insider stock sales Investors are increasingly scrutinizing insider stock sales at Bitcoin miners pursuing AI infrastructure strategies as enthusiasm for the sector cools and governance concerns take center stage. According to Blocksbridge Consulting, executives at TeraWulf, Cipher Digital, Riot Platforms and Core Scientific have disclosed stock sales in recent months, many of them made under prearranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plans. Strategic investors have also trimmed their holdings — including Tether — which reduced its stake in Bitdeer following the company’s AI-driven rally. The shift comes as the TEM AI Infrastructure Growth Index

76022.subject: What Would a Stripe + PayPal Merger Mean for Crypto?
76022.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

**Here are the biggest news in web3:**

* **[Stripe](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/stripe-s-usd53b-paypal-bid-would-combine-bridge-and-pyusd-under-one-owner)**[ makes a $53B bid for PayPal ](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/stripe-s-usd53b-paypal-bid-would-combine-bridge-and-pyusd-under-one-owner)

* **[The DTCC](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/dtcc-starts-live-tokenized-securities-trades-with-more-than-two-dozen-firms)**[ begins live RWA trades with 24+ firms](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/dtcc-starts-live-tokenized-securities-trades-with-more-than-two-dozen-firms)

* **[Ostium](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/ostium-halts-trading-after-oracle-exploit-drains-up-to-usd18m-from-vault)**[ was exploited for ~$18M ](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/ostium-halts-trading-after-oracle-exploit-drains-up-to-usd18m-from-vault)

Two of the biggest names in payments have become two of the biggest names in stablecoins. A takeover bid would put both books under one roof.

**Stripe**, with private-equity firm **Advent International**, made an unsolicited [roughly $53 billion bid for PayPal](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/stripe-s-usd53b-paypal-bid-would-combine-bridge-and-pyusd-under-one-owner). If PayPal accepts, the deal would pair Stripe's **Bridge** and Privy stablecoin infrastructure with PayPal's **PYUSD** token and crypto-trading business, consolidating two mainstream payments companies' stablecoin operations into one.

The bid is a bet on where payments are heading. Stripe bought Bridge to own stablecoin rails; PayPal built PYUSD to issue its own dollar. Combining them would create a payments giant that both issues a stablecoin and runs the infrastructure to move it, the kind of vertical stack competitors would need years to assemble.

**Read more below!**

———————————————————————————

76021.body:
How we measure email
opens, and how to opt out if you'd
like.
sorare-27-competition-strucutre 
( https://clicks.sorare.com/f/a/uIz7yuTM8HvSXqiEicUjLQ~~/AAAHahA~/3N2FVQ5ZydZCmuaE4h9TBEOn7M--LIx_Ctx-bjS2LXDSRRxk4J5WwcqesHDEvYY2vJ54nWsjXT7lVkfhv6InjK5eGh570ZaL0LQ1tom2IfLxQznfDVINHwUgsxS275wu1spSKsxP7T4yXVNfpPMwn6cxDSxKpBMYqX3ofHKw_c0~ )
Hello,

We're writing to inform you of an update to our emailing
practices.

Like most online platforms, the emails we send you may include a
tracking pixel: an invisible technical element that tells us
whether an email has been opened. This helps us better tailor the
content and frequency of our communications to what's actually
useful to you.

We want to leave this choice up to you. Whether or not you accept
this tracking, you'll keep receiving our emails as usual, the
only thing that changes is our ability to know whether you've
opened them.

Manage my
preferences  (
